name: agent-forge
description: Multi-agent engineering system — triage routes user requests to specialist agents (frontend / backend / database / devops / UX / AI), four QC agents fan out for parallel review, and a Ralph Loop auto-fixes findings inside isolated git worktrees. Persistent SQLite state, complexity-based auto model selection (Sonnet ↔ Opus), and a Next.js dashboard with live activity feed.
//commands: Slash commands shipped by this plugin. Claude Code also auto-discovers .claude/commands/*.md; this is the explicit declaration for plugin marketplaces.
//agents: 1st-class agent role definitions with YAML frontmatter. Loaded by the orchestrator at boot via packages/agents/md-loader.ts — not a Claude Code subagent tree.
//services: agent-forge runs two long-lived local services. The plugin manifest is declarative; actual lifecycle is owned by install.sh (initial bring-up) and by the /forge slash command (auto-boot per session).

services.name: orchestrator
services.description: Node daemon — owns DB writes, triage/dispatch, worktrees, Ralph Loop, SSE event source. Sole writer of data/app.db.

services.name: dashboard
services.description: Next.js read-only UI — kanban, decisions, handover FTS5 search, /tasks/[id] live activity feed.

//install: Bootstrap script for fresh machines: pnpm install + migrate + /forge global registration with path substitution. Idempotent.

AGENT_FORGE_MODEL.description: Force a specific model for all dev/QC agents — overrides the per-task auto-tier verdict. e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7' or 'claude-sonnet-4-6'.

AGENT_FORGE_AUTO_TIER.description: 'on' (default, complex → Opus), 'conservative' (same as on), 'eager' (standard also → Opus), 'off' (use each agent's MD-declared model).

RALPH_FINDING_CAP.description: Maximum number of findings Ralph processes per task (severity-ranked). Caps cost.
